signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.4 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains bid; Middle East tensions introduce intervention risk on a break above 160.00. JPY COT LF short −88,543, specs short vs BoJ hike bias, introduces short-squeeze risk. Carry dominates until VIX > 25. Watch for BoJ meeting on 16 Jun to determine yield gap direction.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD/CAD — NZD +0.577% vs CAD -0.606% (spread 1.182%, 7 pairs); RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% — 0bp carry advantage. NZD outperformance driven by strong commodity prices and hawkish RBNZ stance. Watch for RBNZ meeting on 27 May to confirm hike expectations.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7235 — AUD COT LF net long +48,299, specs long vs RBA hike bias, introduces long-unwind risk. Retail 89% short, diverging from specs long, adds to contrarian reversal risk. Watch for RBA meeting on 5 May to determine yield gap direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1755 — EUR COT LF net short -18,293, specs short vs ECB hold bias, introduces short-squeeze risk. HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV at 9.4%, 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. Watch for ECB meeting on 11 Jun to confirm hold expectations.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Cross-Asset Correlation Break
signals.text: GBP/USD vs FTSE 100: corr=+0.346, norm=-0.107, z=+1.27σ. GBP/USD at 1.3595, FTSE 100 at 7,340. Watch for FTSE 100 movement to confirm correlation break.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5955, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, opposed to the RBNZ's hike bias with 40% priced in. This divergence sets up a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling, creating an asymmetric risk. The RBNZ meeting on 27 May will be a key catalyst for this trade.
